F.plain: Best-τ F1 of the ACDC-discovered inter-cell edge set against the exhaustive-oracle true data-flow graph.
F.note: acdc.jl:718-723 (F = best_score.f1); true graph = exhaustive do-set 0/base+17/base+37 over the candidate cells. This is exactly the paper's circuit-discovery F = edge F1 vs the true data-flow graph. Record: extra.triad.F and top-level value (e.g. acdc_pong.json F=0.154; battery mean F=0.625, n=35).

S.formula: S = scrubbing_preserved(discovered_circuit) = fraction of clean candidate-cell readouts unchanged when non-circuit parents are resample-scrubbed
S.plain: Fraction of the clean readouts the discovered circuit alone reproduces when everything outside it is resample-ablated (bit-exact re-run).
S.note: acdc.jl:713-722 (S = scrub_preserved). A held-out-flavoured sufficiency in [0,1] on bit-exact re-runs, but it is a scrubbing-preservation fraction, NOT the paper's held-out PREDICTIVE estimator (fit on a calibration do(u) split, predict a disjoint held-out split vs bit-exact re-run). No calibration/held-out do(u) split; not a correlation in [-1,1]. Record: extra.triad.S (acdc_pong.json S=0.625; battery mean S=0.300, n=35).

M.formula: M = 1 − FP / n_discovered_edges  (1 − over-claim rate at best-τ)
M.plain: One minus the fraction of discovered edges the oracle calls spurious; i.e. edge precision of the discovered circuit.
M.note: acdc.jl:719 (M = 1 − best_score.fp/best_score.n_disc). This is edge PRECISION (over-claim rate), NOT the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat| = true-minimal-cause-set-size / method-named-set-size. For a graph explanation the paper's M would be |true edges| / |discovered edges| (as path_patching does). Record shows M=1.0 (mean M=1.0, n=35) which is precision, not a minimality ratio (recall is very low: only 1 of 12 true edges found in pong).

F.formula: F = 1.0 by construction (recovered == exact); leaderboard passes clamp(1 − |max_abs_recovered−exact|); position record uses F = ρ(recovered_px, exact_px)
F.plain: The patch literally re-runs the exact single-site intervention, so recovered effect equals the oracle effect and the causal-effect agreement is 1.
F.note: activation_patching.jl:582-597 main record (metric max_abs_recovered_minus_exact=0 -> F=1.0); jl:693 position record F = _pearson(rec_px, ex_px). Both are the paper's Phase-C patching F = causal-effect-agreement correlation vs Delta_y(u); here exact by construction. Record: extra.triad.F=1.0 (battery mean F=1.0, n=84).

S.formula: S = fraction of held-out do(u) whose predicted Δy (least-squares a·attr+b fit on a disjoint calibration half) lands within ε of the bit-exact re-run
S.plain: Held-out predictive sufficiency: fit the attribution->Δy map on a calibration half of the causes, predict the disjoint held-out half, count hits within an ε band.
S.note: common/triad_sm.jl:111-155 sufficiency_score, called via triad_extra_dict (activation_patching.jl:597). Exactly the paper's held-out predictive estimator on the oracle's per-cause bit-exact re-runs. Record: extra.triad.S (activation_patching_pong.json S=1.0; battery mean S=0.996, n=84).

M.formula: M = |U*| / |U_hat|,  U* = causes with true |Δy| > mover_floor,  U_hat = above-threshold named causes
M.plain: True minimal cause set size over the method-named set size.
M.note: common/triad_sm.jl:57-86 minimality_score. Exactly the paper's M. Record: extra.triad.M=1.0, M_true_minimal_size / M_named_size (pong |U*|=6/|U_hat|=6; battery mean M=0.973, n=84).

F.formula: F = clamp01( ρ(approx_Δ, exact_Δ) )   (Pearson corr of the linear/gradient approximation vs the exact patch effect)
F.plain: Pearson correlation between the first-order attribution-patching approximation of each patch effect and the exact re-run patch effect.
F.note: attribution_patching.jl:580 (F = clamp(r.corr,0,1)); metric_name corr_approx_vs_exact. This is the paper's Phase-C patching F = causal-effect-agreement correlation vs Delta_y(u). Record: extra.triad.F (attribution_patching_pong.json F=0.876; battery mean F=0.456, n=42).

S.formula: S = fraction of held-out do(u) within ε of the bit-exact re-run (calibration/held-out split of attr->Δy)
S.plain: Same shared held-out predictive sufficiency estimator as activation_patching.
S.note: common/triad_sm.jl sufficiency_score via triad_extra_dict (attribution_patching.jl:580). Paper-conform. Record: extra.triad.S (pong S=0.973; battery mean S=0.769, n=42).

M.plain: True minimal cause set over method-named (above-threshold) set.
M.note: common/triad_sm.jl minimality_score. Paper-conform. Record: extra.triad.M (pong |U*|=6/|U_hat|=5, M=1.0; battery mean M=0.995, n=42).

F.formula: F = scrubbing_preserved_performance_true = fraction of the true-graph sink readouts preserved when every candidate OUTSIDE the true hypothesised circuit is resample-ablated
F.plain: For the ground-truth hypothesised circuit, the fraction of the behaviour (true-graph sink cells) preserved across the resample distribution.
F.note: causal_scrubbing.jl:650-670 (F = clamp(true_preserved)). The hypothesis is built FROM the A1 oracle data-flow (ancestors of the output), so it is oracle-grounded, but F is a scrubbing-preservation fraction, NOT the paper's edge-F1 (circuit discovery) nor a causal-effect-agreement correlation (patching/mediation). It measures 'does the oracle-derived circuit pass the scrub' rather than 'how well does the method's discovered claim agree with the oracle'. Record: extra.triad.F / top-level value (pong F=1.0; battery mean F=0.979, n=42).

S.formula: S = true_preserved (same scalar as F): held-out scrubbing sufficiency = fraction of readouts preserved over held-out resample trials
S.plain: Held-out scrubbing sufficiency; equals the F scalar here.
S.note: causal_scrubbing.jl:659-663 (S = clamp(true_preserved), identical to F). A bit-exact-re-run preservation fraction in [0,1], but NOT the paper's held-out PREDICTIVE do(u) estimator, and S == F leaves S carrying no independent held-out predictive information. Record: extra.triad.S (pong S=1.0; battery mean S=0.979, n=42).

M.plain: True minimal circuit size over the number of candidate cells recorded.
M.note: causal_scrubbing.jl:664 (M = min(1, ncirc/ncand)). Numerator |U*| is paper-correct (true minimal circuit), but the denominator is the fixed CANDIDATE-CELL count (whole recorded state), not the size of the set the METHOD itself NAMES. Since causal scrubbing tests a supplied hypothesis rather than emitting its own named set, U_hat is taken as the candidate pool -> a parsimony-vs-whole-state ratio, close to but not strictly the paper's |U*|/|U_hat|. Record: extra.triad.M (pong |U*|=6/8=0.75; battery mean M=0.430, n=42).

F.formula: F = interchange_accuracy_aligned = fraction of aligned variables whose interchange (swap SOURCE->BASE, re-run) reproduces the high-level abstraction o_V := source(V) at the readout  (= 1.0 by construction)
F.plain: Interchange accuracy of the SUPPLIED alignment: for each aligned cell, does swapping its value reproduce the predicted high-level outcome; 1.0 by construction on the VCS.
F.note: das.jl:638 (F = clamp(iia_aligned)); metric_name interchange_accuracy_aligned. It IS a causal check (each interchange is a real do(cell:=source) bit-exact re-run, so single-cell interchange == the exact patch oracle), but F verifies a SUPPLIED/known-true alignment reproduces the abstraction (Geiger IIA) rather than scoring a method-discovered claim's agreement against {Delta_y(u)} -> '1.0 by construction'. The genuine discovery signal (alignment_accuracy, whether DAS recovers the right cell) is a separate extra field, not F. Record: extra.triad.F=1.0 (battery mean F=1.0, n=42).

S.formula: S = fraction of held-out do(u) within ε of the bit-exact re-run (shared calibration/held-out estimator over the interchange-effect vs exact-effect)
S.plain: Shared held-out predictive sufficiency estimator over the interchange effects.
S.note: common/triad_sm.jl sufficiency_score via triad_extra_dict (das.jl:638, effect vs exact_effect). Paper-conform. Record: extra.triad.S=1.0 (battery mean S=1.0, n=42).

M.plain: True minimal cause set over method-named set.
M.note: common/triad_sm.jl minimality_score. Paper-conform. Record: extra.triad.M (pong |U*|=5/|U_hat|=5, M=1.0; battery mean M=0.890, n=42).

F.formula: LEADERBOARD F = nmf_matched_component_fraction_vs_known_vars (NAMING score vs external labels).   CORRECT causal F = ρ_Spearman(per-atom matched-strength, per-atom ablation causal effect)
F.plain: The leaderboard/site F is the fraction of NMF atoms that align to a known variable label (a naming stat), not agreement with the oracle causal effects.
F.note: dictionaries.jl:1068-1069 sets top-level metric_name=nmf_matched_component_fraction_vs_known_vars, value=nmf matched_component_fraction, and emits NO top-level extra.triad. So leaderboard.py triad_of() returns None and falls back (leaderboard.py:407) to orient_faithfulness(value) => leaderboard F = the MATCHED-COMPONENT FRACTION (dictionaries_pong.json value=0.625; battery mean 0.4405 ~ the paper's 0.44). This is a label-match / naming score against external variable labels, NOT the paper's oracle causal F. The CORRECT causal F IS computed but buried at extra.by_rank[rank==headline_rank].nmf.triad.F = Spearman(matched-strength, ablation causal effect) (dictionaries.jl:756; pong nmf causal F=0.3; battery mean nmf causal F=-0.073 ~ near-zero/negative). Report both: matched fraction 0.44 is a separate naming stat; oracle-agreement causal F ~ 0.10/near-zero.

S.formula: S = ρ_Pearson(per-atom causal effect, per-atom held-out-tail causal effect)   (buried in extra.by_rank[].{pca,nmf,sae}.triad.S)
S.plain: Held-out sufficiency = Pearson correlation of each atom's ablation causal effect with its effect on an unseen continuation (bit-exact re-run). Only in by_rank, not top-level.
S.note: dictionaries.jl:757 (S = _pearson(cu.effect, cu.held_out_effect)). A held-out predictive correlation in [-1,1] (paper-shaped), but computed over a held-out CONTINUATION TAIL rather than a disjoint do(u) split, and NOT surfaced to the leaderboard (no top-level extra.triad). Record: extra.by_rank[headline].nmf.triad.S (pong nmf S=0.932).

M.formula: M = 1 − over-claim rate = 1 − (#matched atoms with ZERO ablation effect) / (#matched atoms)   (buried in by_rank)
M.plain: One minus the fraction of matched atoms whose ablation has no causal effect (present-not-used trap).
M.note: dictionaries.jl:760-762 (M = 1 − overclaim). This is a matched-atom PRECISION / over-claim rate, NOT the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat|. Also only in extra.by_rank[].nmf.triad.M, never at top level (dictionaries has no leaderboard M). Record: by_rank[headline].nmf.triad.M (pong nmf M=1.0).

F.formula: F = clamp01(mean_selectivity),  selectivity = probe_acc − control_task_acc  (Hewitt & Liang), averaged over probeable cells
F.plain: Mean control-task selectivity of the probe (real-task accuracy minus random-label control accuracy), a decodability/PRESENCE signal.
F.note: linear_probing.jl:593 (F = clamp(mean_selectivity)); jl:473 selectivity = pacc − cacc. This scores whether a concept is PRESENT/decodable, NOT agreement with the oracle causal effects {Delta_y(u)}. The paper requires F against the oracle causal effects; probe selectivity is a decodability statistic (present != used), so it is not the paper's causal F. Record: extra.triad.F / value (pong F=0.227; battery mean F=0.108, n=42).

S.plain: Not scored: a probe reads presence, not causal use; it emits no held-out do(u) output prediction.
S.note: linear_probing.jl:594 (S = nothing). Deliberately n/a per paper (present != used, Hewitt & Liang 2019). Record: extra.triad.S = null.

M.plain: Fraction of the probe's named (decodable) cells that are ALSO oracle-causal.
M.note: linear_probing.jl:591-598 (M = min(1, ncausal/ndec)). Denominator |U_hat| = decodable cells the probe names (paper-correct as the method-named set). Numerator uses decodable-AND-causal rather than the size of the TRUE minimal cause set |U*|; it measures naming precision (fraction of named cells that are causal) rather than |U*|/|U_hat|. Directionally aligned but not literally |U*|/|U_hat|. Record: extra.triad.M (pong |U*|=2/|U_hat|=3, M=0.667; battery mean M=0.857, n=32).

F.formula: F = clamp01(logit_lens_fidelity_true_intermediate) = 1.0 by construction (the frozen/identity logit-lens readout IS the cell, so it matches the true intermediate value)
F.plain: Fidelity of the logit-lens readout to the true intermediate variable value; 1.0 by construction because the readout reads the cell directly.
F.note: logit_lens.jl:676 (F = clamp(logit_true_fidelity)); value=1.0. This is readout fidelity to a TRUE INTERMEDIATE value (a positive control by construction), NOT agreement between a method claim and the oracle CAUSAL effects {Delta_y(u)}. Paper's F must be the causal-effect agreement; readout fidelity to the intermediate is a different quantity. Record: extra.triad.F=1.0 (battery mean F=1.0, n=42). (The genuine causal-alignment signal is mean_faithful_weight_fraction, used as M below.)

S.plain: Not scored: the lens reports readout fidelity to the true intermediate, not a held-out do(u) output prediction.
S.note: logit_lens.jl:677 (S = nothing). n/a is defensible per paper. Record: extra.triad.S = null.

M.formula: M = clamp01(mean_faithful_weight_fraction) = mean share of tuned-lens |A_t| readout-weight mass on oracle-causal sites
M.plain: Mean fraction of the tuned-lens readout weight that sits on truly-causal sites (a weight-share proxy, not a cardinality ratio).
M.note: logit_lens.jl:680 (M = clamp(mean_faithful_weight_frac)). A continuous weight-share proxy, NOT the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat| set-cardinality ratio; the lens names a readout DIRECTION not a discrete cell set, so the runner substitutes faithful-weight-fraction. Record: extra.triad.M (pong M=0.438; battery mean M=0.820, n=42).

F.plain: Edge-F1 of the recovered path circuit against the true direct-path routine (exhaustive edge-restricted path patch).
F.note: path_patching.jl:693 (F = clamp(score.f1)); metric_name path_circuit_F1_vs_true_routine. Exactly the paper's circuit-discovery F = edge F1 vs the true data-flow/routine. Record: extra.triad.F / value (pong F=0.0 — no path edges recovered; battery mean F=0.580, n=42).

S.plain: Not scored: the recovered path circuit is a graph structure, not a per-do(u) predictive map.
S.note: path_patching.jl:694 (S = nothing). n/a for a graph-valued explanation is consistent with the paper (S defined for predictive maps). Record: extra.triad.S = null.

M.formula: M = min(1, |true edges| / |discovered edges|)  = |U*| / |U_hat| at the edge level
M.plain: True-routine edge count over the number of discovered path edges.
M.note: path_patching.jl:698 (M = min(1, ntrue/nrec)). This is the paper's |U*|/|U_hat| applied to edges. n/a when |U_hat|=0 discovered edges (emitted as null, e.g. pong M_named_size=0 -> M=null). Record: extra.triad.M, M_true_minimal_size/M_named_size (battery mean M=1.0 over the n=18 games with >=1 discovered edge).

F.formula: LEADERBOARD value = feature_variable_matched_fraction (naming). CORRECT causal F (extra.triad.F) = ρ_Spearman(per-cell SAE recovery = max|feature r|·decoder-mass, oracle causal importance)
F.plain: extra.triad.F is the Spearman correlation between per-cell SAE recovery and the oracle causal importance (paper-shaped); but the top-level leaderboard value is the matched-fraction naming stat.
F.note: sae.jl:775 (F = _spearman(recovery, oracle.importance)) IS oracle-causal-agreement and paper-shaped. BUT sae.jl top-level metric_name=feature_variable_matched_fraction, value=1.0 (a naming score). Because sae DOES emit a top-level extra.triad, leaderboard.py triad_of() reads extra.triad.F (the correct causal Spearman), so the leaderboard F is correct here (sae_pong.json extra.triad.F=0.041; battery mean F~0.002, n=43) — unlike dictionaries whose leaderboard falls back to the naming value. Note the top-level 'value' field is still the misleading matched-fraction.

S.formula: S = ρ_Pearson(per-cell SAE recovery, held-out do(base+37) screen-break effect)
S.plain: Held-out predictive correlation between SAE recovery and the causal effect on an unseen do(base+37) bit-exact re-run; can be negative.
S.note: sae.jl:776 (S = _pearson(recovery, oracle.held_out)). A held-out predictive correlation in [-1,1] on a disjoint intervention (paper-conform; negative allowed). Record: extra.triad.S (sae_pong.json S=−0.458; battery mean S=0.041, n=43).

M.formula: M = mean over causal-use features of (target_share · single-cell-ness)  (monosemanticity proxy)
M.plain: Mean monosemanticity of the causal-use features: how much ablating a feature perturbs ~1 cell AND that cell carries the screen change.
M.note: sae.jl:769-787, score_triad M = mean(target_share · single-cell-ness). This is a monosemanticity/target-share proxy, NOT the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat| cardinality ratio. Record: extra.triad.M (sae_pong.json M=0.039; battery mean M=0.066, n=43).
